headline: In the DRC's Ebola hotspot, a 'cascade of deaths' and fear of contamination
subhead: Le Monde traveled to Mongbwalu, a town in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, where the Ebola virus outbreak began. As the death toll continues to rise, local health facilities and NGOs have become overwhelmed, hampered by a shortage of resources.

headline: US Supreme Court gives Trump the power to fire top officials across the administration – except at the Fed
2026 World Cup: Germany eliminated by Paraguay in stunning upset
France passes watered-down legislation to curb fast fashion
subhead: The law aimed at 'reducing the environmental impact of the textile industry' passed its final reading in the Sénat on June 29. Its content underwent numerous revisions over the past two years, particularly to accommodate domestic players in France, while excluding online giants Shein and Temu from those concessions.

headline: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri and the author of 'Renaissance écologique,' offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.

headline: Six Bad Bunny songs to know before his concerts in France
subhead: The Puerto Rican superstar is set to perform at the Stade-Vélodrome in Marseille on July 1 and at the Plenitude Arena in Nanterre on July 4 and 5.

headline: 'The heatwave shows, beyond any doubt, that without strong mitigation measures, adaptation will be impossible'
subhead: Efforts to combat climate change require far more than mere technical adjustments, writes Le Monde's Stéphane Foucart in his column.

subhead: Produced by Michelle and Barack Obama, the limited series lets Larry David, co-creator of 'Seinfeld,' take a comedic wrecking ball to American mythology.

headline: French research thrown into turmoil by a multifaceted crisis
subhead: France's scientific community faces a host of challenges, from dwindling financial resources, ideological attacks and the emergence of artificial intelligence. The result is worrying for researchers nationwide, at a time when France is also slipping behind in scientific publishing.

headline: From France to Italy, inside the counterfeit rings circulating fake banknotes across Europe via social media
subhead: A joint French-Italian investigation that lasted over a year led to the indictment of 12 people suspected of belonging to a huge counterfeiting network.
